channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Why NFPA 921® And Alarm Science Can Help Identify Responsible Parties In Subrogation Investigations
item.description: View Series NFPA 921® Guide for Fire and Explosion Investigations is the premier, consensus-based forensic standard for scientifically investigating fire incidents. Expert Fire Investigator Steve Carman MSc FPE, IAAI-CFI, CFI, (ATF retired) of Carman & Associates Fire Investigations, Inc, Grass Valley, California, provides this critical insight as to the criticality of having the proper Subject Matter Expert (SME) performing Alarm System Investigations. The Single Data-Bus Circuit Wiring On All Combination Listed Control Units Does Not Conform To UL And NFPA 72 Standards According to Carmen, The data-bus circuit wiring connects to burglar alarm detection sensors, such as motion detectors and audio glass break detectors, zone expansion modules and dialer capture cellular alarm transmitters.

item.title: U.S. Doubles Hormuz Reinsurance Guarantees to $40 Billion With New Partners
item.description: is doubling, to $40 billion, its commitment to provide reinsurance guarantees to ships willing to travel through the Strait of Hormuz with the addition of new insurance partners, including AIG and Berkshire Hathaway. last month announced a $20 billion reinsurance program. On Friday, the agency said Travelers, Liberty Mutual Insurance, Berkshire Hathaway, AIG, Starr and CNA will join Chubb to provide an additional $20 billion in reinsurance for the agency’s maritime facility.

item.title: EPA, HHS to Monitor Drinking Water for Microplastics and Pharmaceuticals
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency and Department of Health and Human Services announced that they will monitor the impact of microplastics and pharmaceuticals on drinking water, the first step toward assessing their health risks and shaping new policies. The EPA will now include microplastics and pharmaceuticals on the sixth Contaminant Candidate List, which would mean they would start being tested and monitored under the Safe Drinking Water Act and receive funding for research, a precursor to future regulation if they are determined to threaten public water systems. Microplastics are microscopic pieces of plastic that have been discovered everywhere from inside human bodies to drinking water to the depths of oceans and Arctic ice.

item.title: Traveling for Business? Don’t Forget to Pack Your Emergency Preparedness Plan
item.description: They have to be educated as to where they’re traveling to have some kind of preparedness ahead of time instead of reacting.” She likened preparedness to packing your suitcase. “Well, you’re going to pack your suitcase, so while you’re at it pack a plan as to what’s going to happen,” she said. And yet, business leaders and other executives still need to travel for business.

item.title: Kaibridge ups A&H capabilities with Melanie Hoffmann hire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Credit Card Backed By Mineral Rights, Flying Ferries, And A Foundation AI Model For Plants
item.description: This is a monthly column that runs down five interesting startup funding deals that may have flown under the radar. Nonetheless, we spotted five companies in just the past month working on issues from cleaner ferries and trains to foundational AI for plants. Frontlands recently raised $50 million in a debt round from StarMesa Capital to provide a credit card to U.S.

item.title: UCITS cat bond funds average 0.35% in March. YTD return now 1.35%: Plenum Index
item.description: Last month, the group of UCITS catastrophe bond funds tracked by Plenum Investments’ Index delivered an average return of 0.46% for February 2026, which was higher than the prior year. On a rolling 12-month return basis to March 27th 2026, the average return across the UCITS catastrophe bond funds tracked by this Index now stands at 10.23%, which has fallen slightly from the end of February 12-month return of 10.46%. UCITS catastrophe bond funds returned 0.35% on average through the period of February 27th to March 27th 2026, which has lifted their year-to-date performance to 1.35%, according to new data from the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices .

item.title: SI Re hires Simon Parten from Schroders Capital as ILS Portfolio Manager
item.description: (SI Re), the Swiss based reinsurer that also invests in insurance-linked securities (ILS) for their diversification and return effects, has hired Simon Parten from Schroders Capital ILS as ILS Portfolio Manager, who will join the company on June 1st, 2026. Parten joins SI Re from Schroders Capital’s ILS business, where he most recently served as Senior Underwriter & Analyst for the firm’s extensive ILS portfolio. In his new role, Parten will help further develop SI Re’s Insurance Linked Securities portfolio, which serves as a key diversifier within the firm’s reinsurance book.

item.title: Pioneer CAT Bond Fund nears $2bn AUM milestone, dedicated ILS fund assets over $5.2bn
item.description: The Victory Pioneer CAT Bond Fund is approaching a new milestone of $2 billion in assets under management, while the combined AUM of the dedicated mutual and institutional insurance-linked securities (ILS) funds managed by the Pioneer Investments team is now over $5.2 billion. These are the two mutual fund strategies with a dedicated catastrophe bond and ILS investment focus that are managed by the Pioneer Investments portfolio management team, which now fall under Victory branding since the acquisition by Victory Capital. Now, the latest reported data from the two dedicated mutual ILS funds portfolio managed by the Pioneer Investments team shows combined assets of just over $2.6 billion as of January 31st 2026.

item.title: ICG hires Luke Schlafly as Global Head of Insurance Client Solutions
item.description: ICG, an alternative asset manager specialist, has appointed Luke Schlafly as Global Head of Insurance within its Client Solutions Group (CSG), reporting to Alan Isenberg, Global Head of CSG. He most recently served as Managing Director and Global Head of Insurance Investment Solutions at PineBridge Investments, where he was responsible for leading the firm’s global insurance business and providing strategic solutions to a diverse client base within this segment. In his new role, Schlafly’s primary focus will be to enhance ICG’s global insurance relationships and create tailored solutions to meet the evolving needs of the sector.

item.title: Critical cloud outage risk remains significant despite decline in occurrence: Parametrix
item.description: Parametrix, a provider of digital business interruption solutions, disclosed in a new whitepaper that although critical cloud service outages declined in 2025 after several years of sustained increases, the risk remains significant. According to the firm’s Cloud Outage Risk Report 2025, after reaching a peak of 244.8 hours in 2024, the total duration of “criticalˮ downtime events, those causing complete or significant service disruption, fell by approximately 28% to 175.3 hours in 2025. The report highlights that despite an overall decrease in downtime, high-impact events persisted with major disruptions affecting some of the most critical cloud regions and services, including GCP outage in us-central1 region in June and the AWS outage us-east-1 in October.

item.title: Arch Insurance North America expands primary cyber coverage to Canada
item.description: Arch Insurance North America has expanded its primary cyber coverage solution, Arch CyPro, to include Canada, effective April 1st, 2026. The cyber coverage, which builds on Arch’s established excess layer cyber capabilities, will be available in Canada through Arch Insurance Canada Ltd. Christopher Gonzales, Vice President, Cyber and Professional Liability Lead, Arch Insurance Canada, commented, “Arch CyPro brings a primary solution to Arch’s existing offerings in Canada at a time of increasingly frequent and sophisticated cyber attacks.

item.title: IEA Chief: Current Oil and Gas Crisis Worse Than 1973, 1979, 2002 Together
item.description: The current oil and gas crisis triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is “more serious than the ones in 1973, 1979 and 2002 together,” Fatih Birol, the head of the International Energy Agency (IEA), told Le Figaro newspaper. “The world has never experienced a disruption to energy supply of such magnitude,” he said in an interview with the French newspaper released in its Tuesday edition. He said the European countries, as well Japan, Australia and others will suffer, but the countries most at risk were developing nations which will suffer from higher oil and gas prices, higher food prices and a general acceleration of inflation.

item.title: Here’s a List of Gulf Energy Infrastructure Damaged in Iran War
item.description: Dozens of refineries, oil fields, gas plants, ports and other energy infrastructure have been damaged by missile and drone strikes, five weeks into the Iran war. Here is a list of some of the Middle East’s most important energy facilities that have been hit or disrupted during the conflict. US President Donald Trump threatened to bring “Hell” to Iran with strikes on power plants and other infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened.

item.title: China Targets Taiwan’s Chip Tech to Evade Global ‘Containment’: Taipei Government
item.description: China is targeting Taiwan to obtain its advanced chip manufacturing technology and talent as a way of breaking through international “containment” of the country, according to a report from the island’s top security agency. China’s scramble for chip talent and expertise has intensified as Beijing pushes for self-reliance in advanced semiconductors, amid a deepening tech rivalry with the U.S. Taiwan, which China claims as its own territory, frequently reports busting networks of Chinese firms illegally trying to poach semiconductor and high-tech talent, and has strict laws to prevent the most advanced technologies going to China.

item.title: DFC and Chubb expand Maritime Reinsurance plan with leading industry partners
item.description: International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) and Chubb have announced the expansion of their Maritime Reinsurance Plan, welcoming a group of prominent U.S. This enhancement increases the total capacity of the Maritime Reinsurance Plan to $40 billion, reinforcing its ability to support international shipping and trade. With the addition of these partners, the facility has been significantly expanded, bringing an additional $20 billion in reinsurance capacity alongside the existing $20 billion in rolling coverage.

item.title: Genasys strengthens board with Hugh Hessing and Gavin Routledge appointments
item.description: Genasys has announced the appointment of Hugh Hessing and Gavin Routledge as Non-Executive Directors, reinforcing its leadership team as the company accelerates its growth as a mid-market challenger in insurance technology. Together with Gavin Routledge, the strengthened board positions Genasys to further enhance its platform capabilities, deepen its market presence, and deliver innovative solutions that empower insurers to operate with greater speed, efficiency, and customer focus. The appointments bring extensive expertise across insurance operations, digital transformation, and strategic scaling, supporting Genasys’ continued expansion and innovation in cloud-based core administration platforms for insurers, MGAs, and brokers.

item.title: WTW powers Thimble pricing
item.description: WTW ’s Radar Live has been selected by Thimble to support commercial lines pricing. WTW worked with Thimble to enhance its existing proprietary technology, constructing a pricing engine with the stability to support rapid growth for the digital-first business. As a result of this collaboration, Thimble achieved full self-sufficiency, with Radar Live enabling its pricing team to derive valuable insights from a wide range of data sources and machine learning models in real time, from a single platform.

item.title: Hugo Launches Independent Agent Channel in 6 Weeks with Zero Manual Compliance Work
item.description: The post Hugo Launches Independent Agent Channel in 6 Weeks with Zero Manual Compliance Work appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Agent onboarding time: Weeks → Minutes with Producerflow Manual compliance tasks per agent: Multiple touchpoints → Zero Support request turnaround: Industry standard days → Under 2 hours Time to implement: 6 weeks The agent channel problem After building a successful direct-to-consumer product, Hugo’s next growth phase depended on expanding into the independent agent channel. Before: Agent channel: Did not exist (D2C only) Compliance infrastructure: None built for agency distribution Team capacity for manual compliance: Near zero given existing workload New onboarding lift for team: Multiple weeks per industry standard The approach Agent program design consultation.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Investments partners with Square Nine Capital
item.description: Liberty Mutual Investments, the investment firm for Liberty Mutual, and Square Nine Capital announced a long-term strategic partnership, focused on supporting the growth of Square Nine as it pursues solutions-oriented corporate credit investments. We are thrilled to partner with Peter and Nicole as they scale Square Nine and pursue attractive opportunities in this overlooked segment.” – John Reichard, Head of Partnerships within Global Credit Markets at Liberty Mutual Investments. “We are excited to partner with Liberty Mutual Investments at this formative stage for Square Nine.

item.title: DKV integra a Ergo Seguros Generales y a Unión Médica la Fuencisla
item.description: DKV Seguros ha formalizado la fusión por absorción de sus filiales Ergo Seguros Generales y Unión Médica la Fuencisla, según consta en el Boletín Oficial del Registro Mercantil (BORME) . Ambas entidades estaban participadas íntegramente por la aseguradora, por lo que la operación se ha articulado mediante un procedimiento simplificado , sin necesidad de ampliación de capital. La integración está orientada a reducir duplicidades administrativas para mejorar la eficiencia, centralizar la gestión y toma de decisiones, y optimizar recursos para invertir y crecer de forma ordenada.

item.title: Santalucía y Sanitas renuevan su alianza en Salud y Decesos
item.description: Santalucía y Sanitas han celebrado una nueva convención conjunta para revisar la evolución de su alianza estratégica y reafirmar su continuidad. Dominique Uzel, director general de negocio de Santalucía, señaló que “la alianza permite anticiparse a las necesidades de los clientes y acompañarlos en los momentos relevantes de su vida”. Javier Ibáñez, director general de Sanitas Seguros, indicó que “la colaboración facilita el uso de herramientas digitales y centros médicos concertados para mejorar la gestión de seguros de Salud y Vida”.

item.title: La prima media en Autos y Motos sube en marzo
item.description: La prima media de los seguros de Autos de nueva producción aumentó un 2% en tasa interanual en marzo, hasta situarse en los 468 euros, según datos de Codeoscopic obtenidos a partir del volumen global del multitarificador Avant2 Sales Manager y analizados mediante tecnología de big data a través de Versus Analytics. En Hogar, la prima media de nueva producción descendió un 3% en marzo . La prima media de los seguros de Moto de nueva producción aumentó un 11% interanual en marzo .

item.title: Lawyers Say up to 75 Women to Benefit From BofA Settlement in Epstein Case
item.description: Lawyers say up to 75 women who were sexually abused by Jeffrey Epstein since 2008 may benefit from a $72.5 million fund set up as part of a settlement Bank of America reached with lawyers representing the women. The judge said he wanted to ensure “nobody is left out.” Related: Preliminary Approval for Bank of America’s $72.5M Settlement With Epstein Accusers Lawyers for women abused by Epstein had sued the bank, saying it ignored suspicious financial transactions involving Epstein that occurred while he was abusing girls and women from June 2008 to his arrest in early July 2019 on federal sex trafficking charges. He also ordered lawyers to submit to him by Friday a broader list of publications to be used to notify Epstein victims that the settlement fund exists.

item.title: FAA Proposes Hiring 2,300 Air Traffic Controllers in Budget Request
item.description: The FAA is about 3,500 fully certified air traffic controllers short of targeted staffing levels. Many controllers are working mandatory overtime and six-day weeks and the FAA’s air traffic control training academy faces serious issues with retaining students. air traffic control system and to boost hiring.

item.title: Stanley Tumblers Maker Prevails in Lawsuit Over Lead Scare
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers of concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead. Litigation over the tumblers began after reports of possible lead contamination went viral on social media in early 2024. But in a 41-page decision, Lin said the plaintiffs did not show the use of lead in Stanley tumblers would be material to reasonable consumers.

item.title: Liberty promotes Celine Choo to head of construction for Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Talbot elevates Jiewei Pang, Russell Giam in Singapore
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Oneglobal Broking promotes Sharron Khoo to Asia COO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zenkyoren launches Guernsey-based reinsurer to diversify risk portfolio
item.description: JA Kyosai (Zenkyoren) has established a reinsurance subsidiary, Zenkyoren Re Limited, in the Bailiwick of Guernsey, a Crown dependency of the United Kingdom. Through this new entity, Zenkyoren aims to support the transfer of risk by International Cooperative and Mutual Insurance Federation (ICMIF) member organisations and others, as well as to accept overseas natural catastrophe risks itself, thereby promoting more diversified risk retention at the Zenkyoren Group level, including through the new reinsurance subsidiary. “Zenkyoren Re will begin by underwriting overseas natural catastrophe risks from ICMIF member organisations and others on a small scale and will gradually expand the size of its portfolio.

item.title: Non-life insurers and reinsurers drives growth at India’s GIFT City
item.description: According to a GIFT city press release, “The growth in premiums has been supported by a steady increase in the number of insurers and reinsurers operating in GIFT City. The number of Insurance Offices (IIOs) has expanded from 8 in the early stages to around 24 currently, reflecting a shift towards a more underwriting-led ecosystem.” In the 2026 financial year, several global insurers and reinsurers marked their entry into GIFT City, including Allianz, Generali, Starr International Insurance , Abu Dhabi National Insurance Company (ADNIC), Qatar Re, Singapore Re, Doha Re, and Lloyd’s , along with domestic players such as HDFC Life Re, Max Life, and Niva Bupa. Ankur Mehta, Managing Director, Guy Carpenter India, commented, “The strong growth in premium volumes reflects increasing participation by global and domestic insurers and the growing confidence in GIFT City as a platform for India-linked risk underwriting.” Atul Boda, Group Chairman, J.B.

item.title: Rokstone expands Singapore marine team with Jackie Lan
item.description: Rokstone, the international specialty re/insurance managing general agent (MGA), has announced the appointment of Jackie Lan as Underwriter in its Singapore marine team. This is the latest key underwriting hire within Rokstone’s Singapore marine team, following the appointments of Sharon Seah and Hui Ling Long in October 2025. Rokstone established its Singapore marine MGA in June 2025 with a multi-year $25 million Lloyd’s capacity deal.

item.title: Connecticut Weighs P/C Insurance Surcharge to Fund Local Infrastructure Resilience
item.description: The surcharge is intended help fund local communities’ efforts to fortify their infrastructure against damage from climate change. Connecticut lawmakers are considering placing a 5% surcharge on commercial property/casualty insurance policies for fossil fuels-related companies at a time when rising insurance premiums and utility bills are concerns. “Now, as risk increases, we must mobilize insurance as a risk reduction mechanism – for the safety and wellbeing of communities, and for the sustainability of the industry.” Rebuild by Design estimates that a 2% surcharge on certain lines of property/casualty insurance in all states could support $287 billion of funding for infrastructure enhancements.

item.title: Yes, Motorist Can Sue for Pothole Injury if the City Knew, Mississippi High Court Says
item.description: No, a municipality is not always immune from negligence claims, and yes, a motorist can sue the city government for injuries caused by an unrepaired pothole if the city was aware of the hazard, the Mississippi Supreme Court concluded last week. But not if agencies have been notified of a dangerous situation and failed to repair it or to warn motorists of the danger, the court said in City of Jackson vs. The trial court judge denied the city’s request for summary judgment, which argued that the city held immunity from torts.

item.title: Maker of Stanley Tumblers Prevails in Lawsuit Over Lead Scare
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers of concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead. Litigation over the tumblers began after reports of possible lead contamination went viral on social media in early 2024. But in a 41-page decision, Lin said the plaintiffs did not show the use of lead in Stanley tumblers would be material to reasonable consumers.

item.title: El costo invisible de no centralizar el antifraude
item.description: Para los departamentos de riesgo en bancos y fintechs, la arquitectura tipo Frankenstein que muchos socios tecnológicos ensamblaron durante años –sistemas unidos que no siempre hablan entre sí– pasó de ser una solución para convertirse en un lastre operativo. La fragmentación está bloqueando la capacidad de respuesta ante un fraude cada vez más sofisticado, y por eso algunas empresas apuestan por plataformas únicas que elimine ese ruido. En un entorno donde el gasto tecnológico consume entre el 6% y el 12% de los ingresos bancarios , operar con cientos de proveedores distintos ya no es un signo de robustez, sino de ineficiencia.

item.title: A-CAP Names Kirk Cullimore Sentinel Security Life President
item.description: (Image source: Kirk Cullimore Utah Senate Update YouTube channel.) Advantage Capital Holdings (Miami) has appointed Kirk Cullimore as President of Sentinel Security Life Insurance Company , effective immediately. “It’s an honor to lead Sentinel Security Life into its next chapter,” Cullimore says, President Sentinel Security Life Insurance Company. “His deep expertise in compliance and governance will accelerate Sentinel’s progress as we embark on the next phase of our long-term growth plan.” Cullimore will oversee operations with an emphasis on enterprise risk management, balance sheet optimization and regulatory engagement as the company expands its position in the life insurance market.

item.title: Insurance leaders highlight data foundations as key to unlocking AI-driven decision power
item.description: The post Insurance leaders highlight data foundations as key to unlocking AI-driven decision power appeared first on Insurtech Insights . A recent roundtable hosted by Insurtech Insights, in collaboration with Amplifi and Stibo Systems, brought together senior insurance leaders to explore how stronger data foundations are accelerating the journey toward AI-powered decision-making. Leaders agreed that continued progress in data foundations will further enhance AI performance, reduce operational complexity, and unlock new opportunities for growth and innovation.

item.title: The Leadership Gap We’re Misreading
item.description: The race to fill senior leadership roles—and to do it faster, more competitively, and with greater diversity—has become a defining challenge for CEOs and CHROs. At the same time, women's advancement into senior leadership continues to lag despite years of focused initiatives and investments. It maps where women are and the movement, but it does not fully illuminate the experience of leadership once they arrive.

item.title: Opendoor to acquire Doma’s escrow operations
item.description: Opendoor announced plans to acquire the closing and escrow operations of Doma , as it moves to expand into real estate closing infrastructure beyond its core home buying business. Doma brings underwriting models and data infrastructure for title risk decisioning, while Opendoor contributes national-scale transaction execution, having facilitated over $100 billion in real estate transactions to date. The move positions Opendoor to play a broader role in the mortgage and refinance process, aiming to streamline closing by combining risk assessment and execution into a single workflow.

item.title: Markel appoints head of U.S. ocean cargo
item.description: Markel has appointed James Shankland as Head of Ocean Cargo, U.S., tasking him with leading the growth of its domestic cargo portfolio. The move signals continued investment in Markel’s global cargo business and an effort to expand its U.S. ocean cargo capabilities, building on its existing ocean marine platform.

item.title: Fuse Advances to Quarter-Finals of Pepperdine University’s 2026 Most Fundable Companies® Competition
item.description: — Media Contact Fuse press@fuserisk.com fuserisk.com The post Fuse Advances to Quarter-Finals of Pepperdine University’s 2026 Most Fundable Companies® Competition appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . New York, NY — April 6, 2026 — Fuse, the AI-powered intelligence synthesis platform for commercial insurance, today announced it has advanced to the Quarter-Finals of Pepperdine University’s 2026 Most Fundable Companies® competition, administered by the Pepperdine Graziadio Business School. That is a fundamentally different product than anything currently available to commercial insurance brokers and carriers.” About the Most Fundable Companies® Competition Now in its tenth year, Pepperdine University’s Most Fundable Companies® competition is one of the most rigorous startup evaluation programs in the United States.

item.title: US Ends Probe into Tesla Remote Driving Feature After Software Updates
item.description: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Monday it closed a probe into nearly 2.6 million Tesla vehicles over a feature allowing users to move cars remotely after finding it was linked only to low-speed incidents. The agency opened the probe into the “Actually Smart Summon” feature in early 2025 after reports of several crashes. Tesla addressed issues through a series of software updates aimed at improving obstacle detection, camera blockage identification and vehicle response to dynamic objects such as gates, the regulator said.

item.title: US Doubles Hormuz Guarantees to $40 Billion With New Partners
item.description: is doubling, to $40 billion, its commitment to provide reinsurance guarantees to ships willing to travel through the Strait of Hormuz with the addition of new insurance partners, including AIG and Berkshire Hathaway. Related: Bessent Says Hormuz Ships Insurance Program to Start Soon The U.S. last month announced a $20 billion reinsurance program.

item.title: Hands-Free Driving Systems Do Not Improve Safety, NTSB Says
item.description: The systems that allow drivers to take their hands off the wheel are convenient but don’t improve safety because people are often too reliant on them and end up paying more attention to their cellphones and infotainment screens than to the road, the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said. Philip Koopman, professor emeritus at Carnegie Mellon University and expert on self-driving vehicle safety, said the NTSB findings highlight longstanding concerns about the limitations of these driver assistance systems and the fact that more advancements are needed. At a March 31 hearing on two fatal crashes involving Ford’s Blue Cruise system, the NTSB recommended that Ford find ways to improve the way its system monitors drivers, and urged the federal government to establish minimum safety standards.

item.title: Mississippi Home Mitigation Bill Heads to Governor in Effort to Improve Storm Resilience
item.description: “I would like to thank the (lawmakers) for their work in coming together on a mitigation program that puts us on a level playing field with other coastal states that have successful mitigation programs such as Alabama and Louisiana,” state Insurance Commissioner Mike Chaney said in a statement Wednesday, adding that his office is committed to “improve long-term resilience for Mississippi homeowners.” Single-family, primary residences anywhere in Mississippi would be eligible for the grants. After nearly two decades of paralysis on the issue, Mississippi officials are inching closer to establishing a state home mitigation program to protect residents from natural disasters. Senate Bill 2409, which would create the Strengthen Mississippi Homes Program, heads to Gov.

item.title: After Software Updates U.S. Safety Agency Ends Probe into Tesla Remote Driving Feature
item.description: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Monday it closed a probe into nearly 2.6 million Tesla vehicles over a feature that allows users to move cars remotely, after finding it was linked only to low-speed incidents. The agency opened the probe into the “Actually Smart Summon” feature in early 2025 after reports of several crashes. NHTSA last month separately upgraded a probe into Tesla’s Full Self-Driving system to an “engineering analysis,” a more advanced stage that typically precedes a potential recall, and expanded the review to about 3.2 million vehicles.

item.title: TikToker Sentenced to 7 Years for Stealing More Than a Dozen Cars Valued at $600,000
item.description: An Arizona man has been sentenced to 7.5 years in prison for committing more than a dozen vehicle thefts throughout the Phoenix metropolitan area, according to a news release published last week by Maricopa County Attorney Rachel Mitchell. Last year, Phoenix Police received a report of a stolen vehicle near 40th Street and Camelback Road in Phoenix. The stolen vehicle had tracking software that allowed police to locate it near McCormick Ranch Golf Club, where they arrested the defendant, Jashwa Hicks.

item.title: US, Iran Study Ceasefire Plan as Deadline Nears on Trump’s ‘Hell’ Threat
item.description: The United States and Iran on Monday were weighing the framework of a plan to end their five‑week-old conflict, even as Tehran pushed back against pressure to swiftly reopen the Strait of Hormuz on the eve of a new ultimatum set by President Donald Trump. demands, such as a 15-point plan, were rejected as “excessive.” Trump will speak about the ceasefire proposal at a press conference at 1 p.m. A senior Iranian official told Reuters that Iran will not reopen the Strait as part of a temporary ceasefire, nor would it accept deadlines or pressure to reach a deal.

item.title: DOXA Buys Florida-Based Jupiter Underwriting Group
item.description: Indiana-headquartered DOXA, a specialty insurance firm built on acquiring and growing program administrators and MGAs, has expanded into Florida with the acquisition of Jupiter Underwriting Group. Jupiter has focused on underwriting complex risk for contractors, hospitality firms, real estate, import/export companies and light manufacturing and oil and gas contractors. Topics Mergers & Acquisitions Florida Underwriting
